source_policy.primary_source_meaning: The most developed current documentation or governance surface reviewed for this repository; it is not automatically accepted, canonical, merge-ready, or authoritative.
source_policy.correction_rule: Replace or qualify an entry only through a new exact-source review that preserves the superseded tuple and explains the change.
source_policy.withdrawal_rule: Withdrawn candidates remain preserved as historical evidence and must not remain current registry entries.
source_policy.rollback_rule: Revert this profile and preserve its exact source generation if any entry is shown to be incorrect; do not activate an owner, route, namespace, capability, release, publication, or deployment as rollback.

repositories.candidate_responsibility: constitutional charter, decision packets, portfolio authority matrix, governance sequencing, and non-authorizing review evidence
conflicts: D1 canonical identity remains unresolved against aevespers2/Alistaire-agi
repositories.required_disposition: approve, revise, or withdraw the charter candidate through D1-D5 without treating documentation as authority

repositories.candidate_responsibility: identity review, compatibility landing, migration, placeholder-tree governance, and possible non-canonical role
conflicts: PR #4 documentation front door and PR #2 consolidation lineage overlap
product identity overlaps ALISTAIRE-
repositories.required_disposition: preserve both candidate lineages and decide canonical, compatibility, split-role, or retirement treatment in D1

repositories.candidate_responsibility: portable bootstrap, observation, proposal, separately authorized reversible execution, verification, and evidence
conflicts: primary documentation and portable-contract candidate is no longer mergeable against current main
multiple product and security lineages remain active
repositories.required_disposition: reconcile current main and competing lineages before any portable-trust contract claim is current

repositories.candidate_responsibility: independent quarantine, capability, disposition, revocation, checkpoint, correction, recovery, and conformance evidence
conflicts: documentation and conformance PR #2 coexists with executable security PR #1

repositories.candidate_responsibility: incubation and specialist PhantomBlock host-observation research

repositories.candidate_responsibility: declarative genome identity, lineage, immutable policy, compatibility, projection, capability-evidence, and bounded self-edit review

PR #2 remains conflicted and carries retired identity semantics

repositories.candidate_responsibility: reference conformance, deterministic replay comparison, and kernel-to-runtime crosswalk documentation
conflicts: internal QSIO ledger has no accepted mapping to runtime event or execution-report records

repositories.required_disposition: reconcile runtime, preflight, security, and documentation lineages before selecting interface semantics

repositories.candidate_responsibility: bounded multi-QSO composition, experiment orchestration, QSO format and conversion candidates, projection receipts, and aggregate evidence

repositories.candidate_responsibility: source acquisition boundary, sanitization, attribution, provenance, rights/privacy review, and bounded artifact handoff
conflicts: PR metadata head differs from the exact head declared in the PR body
collection, spawning, orchestration, and QSIO lineages remain separate
repositories.required_disposition: correct the stale exact-head claim and revalidate the actual candidate before further integration

repositories.candidate_responsibility: temporal assessment, freshness, ordering, replay, operational-state and evidence separation, and integrity review

PR #3 is stacked on PR #1 without exact-head workflow evidence

portfolio_vacancies.role: canonical charter and repository identity owner
neutral contract, schema, namespace, fixture, and compatibility steward
canonical bytes, digest domains, identity primitives, time, replay, and extension owner
independent issuer, approver, revoker, key custodian, disposition, and recovery authority
portfolio incident commander, evidence custodian, freeze, restart, rollback, invalidation, and claim-withdrawal owners
runtime event and execution-report semantic owners
Fabric projection, collaboration-event, aggregate-report, and route owner
source-rights, privacy, retention, correction, deletion, legal-hold, and publication owners
review-contract, accessibility-certification, and independent approval owners
financial and engineering adapter authorization, revocation, incident, and recovery owners

dissent.meaning: Structural conflicts, contradictory candidate claims, stale metadata, and ownership vacancies were found. They are not attributed to a human reviewer as dissent without an exact review record.

gluing_obstructions: multiple active lineage without accepted precedence
source-rights, privacy, retention, and publication ownership vacancy
review, financial, and engineering authorization ambiguity
correction, revocation, migration, rollback, and restoration discontinuity
acceptance_sequence: correct stale source identities and revalidate exact candidate heads
preserve and classify sibling lineages as current, historical, superseded, withdrawn, blocked, or unsupported
record verified human dissent separately from structural conflict
complete D1-D5 decisions and accept owners or explicit vacancies
select qualified record families, namespaces, canonical bytes, source sets, receipts, ordering, replay, correction, revocation, privacy, retention, migration, rollback, and restoration rules
run independent pairwise and triple-overlap fixtures
verify resulting repository state without expanding implementation authority

fysa_120.proposed_subdivision: 013-I — Cross-repository authority-currentness, conflict, dissent, and vacancy reconciliation
fysa_120.authority_boundary: Skill selection and artifact validation do not establish competence, appointment, ownership, permission, acceptance, or authority.
